---
title: "Receptor Scan: Cell-Type Comparison"
---

# Summary
Overlays receptor scan results for multiple target cell types on a single metric
scatter plot. Each receptor pair appears once per cell type as a point colored by
cell type, with no thresholding or labeling. Useful for visually assessing whether
the metric landscape shifts across cell types and which pairs score consistently
high or are cell-type-specific.

# Inputs
- Two CSV files produced by `run_selectivity_scan` or `run_KL_EMD_scan`
  (may be the same file to compare two metrics within it); matching `.yaml` files must exist
- `SCAN_PATH_1`, `SCAN_PATH_2`: paths to those CSVs
- `KL_METRIC_1`, `KL_METRIC_2`: for KL/EMD scans, selects which method to use per path — `"KL_Divergence"` or `"EMD"`; metrics for selectivity scans are derived automatically
- `AXIS_1_LABEL`, `AXIS_2_LABEL`: human-readable axis labels; set to `None` to auto-derive from the metric name
- `TARG_CELL_TYPES`: list of target cell types to extract and overlay

# Outputs
- Scatter plot: x = metric 1, y = metric 2
  - One color per cell type; all receptor pairs plotted as equal-sized points

```{python}
%config InlineBackend.figure_formats = ['svg']
import os

import matplotlib.lines as mlines
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import yaml

# ── Parameters ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SCAN_PATH_1 = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_selec_scan_full.csv"
SCAN_PATH_2 = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_dist_scan_full_filt.csv"
SCAN_PATH_1 = (
    SCAN_PATH_2  # For comparing two metrics within the same scan, set paths equal.
)

# For KL_EMD scans, selects which method to use as the axis metric.
KL_METRIC_1 = "EMD"  # "KL_Divergence" or "EMD"
KL_METRIC_2 = "KL_Divergence"

# Human-readable axis labels; set to None to auto-derive from the metric name.
AXIS_1_LABEL = None
AXIS_2_LABEL = None


# ── Derive metrics from YAML ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _scan_metric(scan_path, kl_metric):
    yaml_path = os.path.splitext(scan_path)[0] + ".yaml"
    with open(yaml_path) as f:
        scan_type = yaml.safe_load(f)["scan_type"]
    return "Selectivity" if scan_type == "selectivity" else kl_metric


METRIC_1 = _scan_metric(SCAN_PATH_1, KL_METRIC_1)
METRIC_2 = _scan_metric(SCAN_PATH_2, KL_METRIC_2)
AXIS_1_LABEL = AXIS_1_LABEL or METRIC_1.replace("_", " ")
AXIS_2_LABEL = AXIS_2_LABEL or METRIC_2.replace("_", " ")

# List of target cell types to overlay; each is plotted in a distinct color.
TARG_CELL_TYPES: list[str] = [
    "Treg",
    "CD4 TEM",
    "CD4 CTL",
    "CD4 Naive",
    "CD8 TEM",
    "dnT",
    "ILC",
    "NK_CD56bright",
]
TARG_CELL_TYPES = None

POINT_SIZE = 12
POINT_ALPHA = 0.35
AXIS_NORM = "z-score"  # "none", "z-score", "rank"


# ── Load scans ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def load_scan_flat(path, metric_col, cell_type):
    """Return DataFrame with columns rec1, rec2, metric for the given cell type."""
    raw = pd.read_csv(path)
    assert cell_type in raw["Cell_Type"].values, f"{cell_type!r} not found in {path}"
    assert metric_col in raw.columns, f"{metric_col!r} not a column in {path}"
    sub = raw[raw["Cell_Type"] == cell_type][
        ["Receptor_1", "Receptor_2", metric_col]
    ].copy()
    return sub.rename(
        columns={"Receptor_1": "rec1", "Receptor_2": "rec2", metric_col: "metric"}
    ).reset_index(drop=True)


# ── Resolve cell types ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if TARG_CELL_TYPES is None:
    ct1 = set(pd.read_csv(SCAN_PATH_1)["Cell_Type"].unique())
    ct2 = set(pd.read_csv(SCAN_PATH_2)["Cell_Type"].unique())
    TARG_CELL_TYPES = sorted(ct1 & ct2)

# ── Load all cell-type data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_ct_dfs = {}
for cell_type in TARG_CELL_TYPES:
    scan1 = load_scan_flat(SCAN_PATH_1, METRIC_1, cell_type)
    scan2 = load_scan_flat(SCAN_PATH_2, METRIC_2, cell_type)
    _merged = (
        pd.merge(scan1, scan2, on=["rec1", "rec2"], suffixes=("_1", "_2"))
        .rename(columns={"metric_1": "s1", "metric_2": "s2"})
        .dropna(subset=["s1", "s2"])
    )
    assert len(_merged) > 0, f"No valid receptor pairs found for {cell_type!r}"
    _merged["cell_type"] = cell_type
    _ct_dfs[cell_type] = _merged

# ── Apply normalization globally across all cell types ─────────────────────────
_all = pd.concat(_ct_dfs.values(), ignore_index=True)
if AXIS_NORM == "z-score":
    _s1_mean, _s1_std = _all["s1"].mean(), _all["s1"].std() + 1e-9
    _s2_mean, _s2_std = _all["s2"].mean(), _all["s2"].std() + 1e-9
    _all["s1"] = (_all["s1"] - _s1_mean) / _s1_std
    _all["s2"] = (_all["s2"] - _s2_mean) / _s2_std
elif AXIS_NORM == "rank":
    _all["s1"] = _all["s1"].rank(method="average")
    _all["s2"] = _all["s2"].rank(method="average")

_norm_suffix = {"none": "", "z-score": " (z-score)", "rank": " (rank)"}[AXIS_NORM]
AXIS_1_LABEL = AXIS_1_LABEL + _norm_suffix
AXIS_2_LABEL = AXIS_2_LABEL + _norm_suffix

# ── Plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_cmap = plt.colormaps["tab20"]
cell_type_colors = {
    ct: _cmap(i / max(len(TARG_CELL_TYPES) - 1, 1))
    for i, ct in enumerate(TARG_CELL_TYPES)
}

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))

for cell_type in TARG_CELL_TYPES:
    df = _all[_all["cell_type"] == cell_type]
    ax.scatter(
        df["s1"],
        df["s2"],
        s=POINT_SIZE,
        alpha=POINT_ALPHA,
        color=cell_type_colors[cell_type],
        linewidths=0,
        zorder=2,
    )

# Highlight the top-selectivity receptor pair for each cell type
_top_pairs = _all.loc[_all.groupby("cell_type")["s1"].idxmax()]
for _, row in _top_pairs.iterrows():
    ax.scatter(
        row["s1"],
        row["s2"],
        s=POINT_SIZE * 10,
        marker="*",
        color=cell_type_colors[row["cell_type"]],
        edgecolors="black",
        linewidths=0.5,
        zorder=5,
    )

_top_pairs = _all.loc[_all.groupby("cell_type")["s2"].idxmax()]
for _, row in _top_pairs.iterrows():
    ax.scatter(
        row["s1"],
        row["s2"],
        s=POINT_SIZE * 10,
        marker="*",
        color=cell_type_colors[row["cell_type"]],
        edgecolors="black",
        linewidths=0.5,
        zorder=5,
    )

handles = [
    mlines.Line2D(
        [0],
        [0],
        marker="o",
        color="w",
        markerfacecolor=cell_type_colors[ct],
        markersize=8,
        label=ct,
    )
    for ct in TARG_CELL_TYPES
]
handles.append(
    mlines.Line2D(
        [0],
        [0],
        marker="*",
        color="w",
        markerfacecolor="gray",
        markeredgecolor="black",
        markeredgewidth=0.5,
        markersize=12,
        label="Top selectivity pair",
    )
)
# ax.legend(
#     handles=handles, title="Cell type", fontsize=9, title_fontsize=10, framealpha=0.8
# )

ax.set_xlabel(AXIS_1_LABEL, fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel(AXIS_2_LABEL, fontsize=13)
types_str = (
    ", ".join(TARG_CELL_TYPES)
    if len(TARG_CELL_TYPES) <= 3
    else f"{len(TARG_CELL_TYPES)} cell types"
)
ax.set_title(
    f"{AXIS_1_LABEL} vs. {AXIS_2_LABEL}\nTargets: {types_str}",
    fontsize=12,
    pad=12,
)
ax.tick_params(labelsize=11)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
from scipy.stats import spearmanr

rows = []
for ct in TARG_CELL_TYPES:
    sub = _all[_all["cell_type"] == ct]
    r, p = spearmanr(sub["s1"], sub["s2"])
    rows.append({"Cell Type": ct, "Spearman r": round(r, 4), "p-value": f"{p:.2e}"})

overall_r, overall_p = spearmanr(_all["s1"], _all["s2"])
rows.append(
    {
        "Cell Type": "Overall",
        "Spearman r": round(overall_r, 4),
        "p-value": f"{overall_p:.2e}",
    }
)

corr_df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
print(f"Spearman correlation: {AXIS_1_LABEL} vs. {AXIS_2_LABEL}\n")
print(corr_df.to_string(index=False))
```
